prompt: Summarize the latest thinking on retrieval-augmented generation and show me a diagram of how it works.

stages.title: Input
stages.annotation: When someone hits send, the server gets their message, the model they chose, which tools are enabled, and any files they attached.

artifact.rawMessage: Summarize the latest thinking on retrieval-augmented generation and show me a diagram of how it works.

stages.title: Context load
stages.annotation: The server assembles a compact context pack: recent turns plus a rolling summary. Older turns stay in the UI but are omitted from the model window when they would blow the token budget.

artifact.incomingMessage: Summarize the latest thinking on retrieval-augmented generation and show me a diagram of how it works.

excluded.label: Turns 1–3
excluded.reason: Outside last-N window; summary already captures intent.

excluded.label: Attached PDF excerpt
excluded.reason: User disabled file grounding for this thread.

stages.title: Memory search
stages.annotation:
Mem0-style retrieval runs against persisted memories.

High-relevance snippets are injected into the system context.

Low-score or stale rows are dropped so the answer stays grounded and small.

injected.memory: User prefers concise technical explanations with diagrams when learning new ML topics.
User is comparing RAG architectures for a product doc and cares about latency vs quality.

filteredOut.memory: User once asked for a plain-text summary of RAG without diagrams; low relevance to this diagram-heavy query.
Older note: user wanted bullet lists for RAG tradeoffs (latency vs quality) from a prior thread.
filteredOut.reason: Superseded by newer memory on same topic

stages.title: Tool routing
stages.annotation: The model emits parallel tool calls: refresh the public literature with web search, then materialize structure as a Mermaid diagram. Each call is validated before execution.

artifact.modelNotes: User asked for latest thinking (web) and a diagram (Mermaid). Memory already loaded; run web_search then make_mermaid_diagram.

arguments.query: retrieval augmented generation survey 2025 2026 agentic RAG

arguments.description: Flowchart: user query through embedding, retrieval, rerank, injection, LLM answer.

stages.title: Token budget & streaming
stages.annotation: Streaming keeps time-to-first-token low while a completion budget protects cost. A short system reminder nudges the model to land cleanly before the buffer runs out.

artifact.gracefulFinishInstruction: You are near the end of the allotted completion length. Conclude with at most two short paragraphs and avoid opening new tool calls.

sources.title: Retrieval-Augmented Generation for Knowledge-Intensive NLP Tasks
sources.snippet: Introduces RAG as a general-purpose fine-tuning method…

sources.title: Agentic Retrieval-Augmented Generation: A Survey
sources.snippet: Surveys planner–executor loops that iterate retrieval…

stages.title: Structured card render
stages.annotation: Tool JSON becomes first-class UI: Exa rows as glass cards, memory hits as scored rows, and Mermaid as an acknowledged diagram plus inline rendering in markdown.

artifact.rawMarkdown:
## RAG in one pass

Modern **retrieval-augmented generation** couples a parametric LM with a non-parametric index. Agentic variants add planning loops, but the core contract stays: *retrieve → compress → condition → generate*.

```mermaid
flowchart LR
  Q1["User query"] --> E1["Embed"]
  E1 --> R1["Retrieve top K"]
  R1 --> X1["Rerank and filter"]
  X1 --> I1["Inject into prompt"]
  I1 --> L1["LLM"]
  L1 --> A1["Answer"]
```

The diagram mirrors the tool-generated flowchart so you can compare raw fences with the diagram card.

memories.memory: User prefers concise technical explanations with diagrams when learning new ML topics.
User is comparing RAG architectures for a product doc and cares about latency vs quality.

results.title: Retrieval-Augmented Generation for Knowledge-Intensive NLP Tasks
highlights: Introduces RAG as a general-purpose fine-tuning method that combines pre-trained parametric and non-parametric memory.
Achieves state-of-the-art results on open-domain Natural Questions without expensive intermediate latent retrieval representations.

results.title: Agentic Retrieval-Augmented Generation: A Survey
highlights: Surveys planner–executor loops that iterate retrieval until a stopping criterion is met.
Discusses grounding risks when tools chain without tight budget controls.

displayBody.code:
flowchart TD
  Qd["Query"] --> Ed["Embed query"]
  Ed --> Rd["Retriever top K"]
  Rd --> Fd["Filter and rerank"]
  Fd --> Pd["Prompt assembly"]
  Pd --> Md["LLM"]
  Md --> Od["Grounded answer"]

stages.title: Optional TTS
stages.annotation: For read-aloud, citations and code fences are softened into speakable prose. Playback uses the same glass dock styling as chat, but audio here is a short pre-rendered clip—no ElevenLabs call in the browser.

artifact.speechFriendlyBefore:
## RAG in one pass

Modern **retrieval-augmented generation** ... ```mermaid
flowchart LR
...```
artifact.speechFriendlyAfter: RAG in one pass. Modern retrieval augmented generation couples a language model with an external index. The diagram branch reads: query, embed, retrieve top results, rerank, inject into the prompt, then answer.

finalResponse.markdown:
## RAG in one pass

Modern **retrieval-augmented generation** couples a parametric LM with a non-parametric index. Agentic variants add planning loops, but the core contract stays: *retrieve → compress → condition → generate*.

```mermaid
flowchart LR
  Q1["User query"] --> E1["Embed"]
  E1 --> R1["Retrieve top K"]
  R1 --> X1["Rerank and filter"]
  X1 --> I1["Inject into prompt"]
  I1 --> L1["LLM"]
  L1 --> A1["Answer"]
```

The diagram mirrors the tool-generated flowchart so you can compare raw fences with the diagram card.
